The business cycle framework itself may not apply — the U.S. may be in a fiscal dominance regime where NBER business cycle dating conventions are misleading.

Growth is fundamentally demand-determined through hysteresis — potential GDP follows actual GDP, not vice versa (strong form: output gap is theoretically incoherent).
Treasury debt maturity structure functions as an implicit government growth forecast — the barbell approach signals confidence in sustained nominal GDP.

Immigration is being double-counted: it cannot simultaneously raise potential GDP AND keep the labor market loose enough to prevent wage inflation — creating an internal inconsistency in the consensus narrative.
Gig/contractor W-2 to 1099 reclassification flatters operating margins without changing economic costs
US rate hikes may paradoxically support domestic labor demand on net via strong dollar terms-of-trade effect (cheap imports → consumer purchasing power → sustained labor demand)
Sectoral wage dispersion (services ~4.5-5% vs. goods ~3-3.5%) creates a within-quadrant factor rotation signal where quality/low-labor-intensity factors outperform when dispersion is wide
AI displacement combined with immigration restriction creates a segmented labor market (white-collar excess, blue-collar shortage) with sticky services inflation despite stable headline unemployment
Japan's Shunto wage dynamics are the most important structural FX story in G10 — if 5%+ wage growth sustains, BoJ normalization narrows USD/JPY carry structurally
Remote work creates Dutch Disease dynamics in small EM economies where USD-denominated income bids up skilled labor costs, crowding out local tradables

QE sustained financial asset inflation rather than productive investment, evidenced by flat-to-declining gross fixed capital formation/GDP during record-low real rates.

Genuine normalization succeeding (balance sheet shrinkage to $4-5T via AI-driven productivity boom, fiscal consolidation, or regulatory reform) is the truly under-explored contrarian tail that almost no one is positioned for.

Kalecki profit equation constrains corporate debt serviceability during QT — if fiscal deficits narrow while investment is weak, corporate cash flows deteriorate mechanically, exacerbating Minsky fragility.
Central bank put is measurable via MOVE-VIX ratio (~6-7x vs historical ~4-5x), reflecting inconsistent pricing of the policy backstop across equity and rates vol markets.

The Ample Reserves framework is a post-hoc rationalization — the Fed backed into it because the corridor system failed in September 2019, not from first-principles derivation. The framework is circular and untested under adverse conditions.

A dollar structural weakening cycle has begun following the 2022 peak, following historical 7-10 year phase patterns.
EM currency regime classification (managed vs free float) explains a meaningful portion of the 2-4x P/E valuation gap between comparable EM markets.
Forward guidance credibility erosion contributes an additional 1-2 vol points of FX gamma underpricing beyond carry-driven suppression.
A three-tier currency regime fragmentation is emerging: dollar bloc, CNY-anchored bloc, and freely floating middle tier absorbing adjustment stress.
The PBOC administered FX regime accumulates stored energy: a growing gap between administered and shadow equilibrium rates that eventually produces discontinuous adjustment.

Fiscal dominance could bifurcate the Dollar Smile left tail, producing an unprecedented anti-dollar risk-off where the dollar weakens simultaneously with equities during a US-origin fiscal crisis.
The next EM currency crisis will likely originate with a commodity exporter rather than importer, driven by Dutch Disease dynamics and fiscal complacency.
Maturity-dependent correlation bifurcation (2Y-SPX negative, 30Y-SPX slightly positive) means carry unwind hedges using long-end duration will fail asymmetrically. This is a post-2022 phenomenon.

Augmentation-to-substitution ratio at 1.0-1.3:1 places AI in historically ambiguous labor zone with time-varying trajectory

Private credit spillover operates through Godley sectoral balance (flow-of-funds) disruption rather than price contagion, transmitting chronically over quarters-to-years.
HY spread widening equity market impact threshold
The credit impulse-to-equity R-squared has experienced a structural break from 0.38 to 0.12, reflecting genuine post-GFC funding shift rather than extended lag structure.
Credit and equity market composition has structurally decoupled: top 10 S&P 500 hold ~$500B net cash with zero Merton linkage, while HY issuers are $2-5B market-cap leveraged companies.
A yield-curve-mediated contagion sequence operates as: inversion → bear steepener → credit blowout → equity drawdown.
Multi-step contagion chains complete only ~15-25% of the time (median ~38%), with policy intervention as primary chain-breaker.
CRE-to-bank-equity contagion mechanism
Private credit-to-public market spillover channels

The Kalecki fiscal buffer raising the HY threshold by 100-150bp is itself destabilizing because more credit damage accumulates before equities register the problem — analogous to suppressing small forest fires.
ROIC-WACC spread trajectory can serve as a diagnostic for distinguishing first-round from second-round commodity-inflation effects.
Second-round effects vs first-round price level shifts
The passthrough coefficient may have structurally re-risen post-COVID due to firms learning they can raise prices without losing volume.
Petrodollar recycling creates a structural self-hedging asymmetry for the US during oil shocks via GCC SWF Treasury purchases (r≈0.6 correlation with oil prices).
The dollar cycle is approaching an inflection point based on twin-deficit fundamentals (~10% GDP combined) and declining foreign UST purchases (50% to 25% of issuance).

The green energy transition will structurally change commodity-inflation transmission dynamics, shifting from oil/gas to metals (copper, lithium, rare earths) with different supply elasticities and financialization characteristics.

OCF/revenue growth ratio with 1.0x threshold is the firm-level commodity passthrough diagnostic.
Dedollarization is gradually increasing commodity-inflation passthrough variance across countries.
Energy price passthrough lags by sector
Gold model residual ($900-1,100/oz above real-rate fair value) serves as a quantitative measure of FX system fragmentation, correlating r=0.80 with cumulative central bank gold purchases since 2022.

Caloric Concentration Index (weighting food imports by caloric contribution rather than dollar value) is a more predictive FX fragility indicator than aggregate food import ratios, with 2-3x higher FX volatility for concentrated importers.

EM carry should be decomposed into pure carry (2-3%), sovereign credit (2-4%), convertibility risk (1-3%), and liquidity premium (1-2%), with 40-60% of total return attributed to credit/institutional risk.
OPEC+ spare capacity levels below 2mb/d interact with carry fragility by determining energy-FX shock amplitude, creating a three-factor conjunction risk.
The fertilizer-FX nexus is a first-order missing variable in FX models, where regional gas price differentials create divergent fertilizer cost structures affecting crop production costs and FX.
Carry factor crowding is currently elevated in the top quartile of historical observations, with 60% conditional probability of negative carry returns.
PGM tightening as contrarian ZAR support in H2 2026. Palladium shifted from ~400koz surplus to ~100-200koz deficit. If PGM prices rally 15-25%, this adds ~$3.6bn annualized export revenue (0.9% GDP).
De-dollarization flows at $380-575bn/year across gold purchases ($85-95bn), bilateral swap utilization ($80-120bn), and non-USD invoicing shift ($215-360bn).
Agricultural inflation as hidden factor exposure explaining 10-20% of pure carry residual variance in EM portfolios, via food CPI endogeneity to EM rate-setting.
Biofuel mandates create a meaningful price floor that extends food inflation persistence during carry stress, fundamentally altering commodity-FX dynamics.
Commodity ToT as a fourth FX factor candidate achieving t≥3.0 orthogonal to carry + momentum in commodity-exporter subsamples, with non-linear 2-3x amplification.
EM carry trade risk in tightening DM cycles
Gold residual ($900-1,100/oz above real-rate model) signals FX-regime fragmentation, correlating with EM central bank purchases at r=0.80.
Hold-phase FX dynamics follow a bimodal pattern determined by credibility of the hold, with median -8% FX move over 6 months during hold-to-cut transitions.
CIP basis is an investable factor with implementable Sharpe ratio of 0.2-0.4.

Divergence resolution follows different patterns based on type: single-outlier episodes resolve primarily through FX adjustment (~75%) versus multi-bank divergence resolving through rate convergence (~60%).

Private credit elasticity to term premium determines compressed spring duration. If private credit ($2T+ AUM, partially insulated from public rates) continues intermediating despite rising term premia, the compressed spring extends.
Cross-asset dimensionality collapse indicator with 1-3 day lead time, averaging effective dimensionality across FX, credit, and equity factor spaces.
Fed regime conditions ALL FX factor returns (carry, value, momentum) — not just carry — with statistically different return distributions across Fed policy regimes.

Copper structural deficit (0.5-1.5mt by 2027-2030) driving FX beta regime change as copper transitions from China-construction-beta to electrification-beta. Chile export revenue impact of $15-25bn/year at $12,000-15,000/t.
Export ban contagion is the most under-modeled tail risk in FX-rates divergence, with game-theoretic cascade mechanics across food-exporting nations.
Agricultural seasonality creates a predictable 'carry fragility window' during Northern Hemisphere growing season (June-August).
Dollar super-cycles have predictive value based on n=6 observed cycles with lengths of 7, 7, 10, 7, 9, 11 years.
Central bank divergence currency implications
Rate differential FX impact under different vol regimes
Simultaneous unwind of FX-unhedged foreign Treasury holdings (~$8T) and domestic basis trades ($800B-$1T) could invert the flight-to-quality pattern for US Treasuries.
Seasonal crop calendar concentrates carry tail risk in predictable June-August windows, making this exploitable for hedging.

PBOC/CNY competitive devaluation would amplify BoJ normalization into a regional FX crisis via the chain: BoJ normalizes, JPY strengthens, Chinese exporters gain share, PBOC allows CNY depreciation, triggering Asian EM contagion.

FX-unhedged Treasury holdings by Japanese institutions create dual fragility with the domestic basis trade, risking a pro-cyclical doom loop in the benchmark safe asset under a Fed-cuts/BoJ-hikes scenario.

Structural vs cyclical divergence discriminant using output gap, 5Y-5Y forwards, and structural current account achieves 75% cross-validated accuracy on 8 episodes, with implied FX magnitudes of ~10-15% for cyclical and ~20-35% for structural.
ENSO as forecastable but unpriced driver of agricultural-exporter FX vol, with AUD/USD drawdowns during strong El Niño averaging 8-12% beyond rate-differential predictions.
